food_subgroup: Hot and cold coffee beverages with milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Nanyang iced coffee with condensed and evaporated milk. FOODON_03301710 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Coffee, with milk) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Nanyang-style iced coffee with condensed and evaporated milk.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 (Coffee substance) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific concept exists for Nanyang iced coffee preparation.

main_ingredients: Robusta coffee beans (dark roasted, butter/sugar-roasted Nanyang style)

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of Ice Kopi as served at a Singaporean kopitiam or Toast Box outlet, approximately 400 ml, containing brewed Nanyang coffee, condensed milk, evaporated milk, and ice.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi (Coffee with Condensed Milk and Evaporated Milk) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists standard Kopi (hot, ~200 ml serving) with condensed and evaporated milk. Values are adapted to per-100g basis. The large iced serving (400 ml) dilutes the beverage slightly with ice melt; per-100g values are estimated from the HPB standard Kopi composition, adjusted for the typical ice-dilution factor (~10–15%) observed in iced kopitiam beverages. USDA FoodData Central (condensed milk, evaporated milk, brewed coffee) used as cross-reference for macro validation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened condensed milk and coffee-milk beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nanyang-style iced kopi with condensed and evaporated milk. GI estimated at 41 (low) based on: (1) sweetened condensed milk GI ~61 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) brewed coffee has negligible glycemic contribution; (3) evaporated milk GI ~30–35; (4) weighted average of milk components in a standard kopi formulation (~30 ml condensed milk + ~20 ml evaporated milk per 200 ml serving), diluted by ice. The composite GI of approximately 41 is consistent with the low-GI category. GL per large serving (400 g, carbohydrate_g ~32.8 g total): GL = 41 × 32.8 / 100 = 13.4.

hypertension.guidance: A large Ice Kopi contains approximately 112 mg sodium per serving; while moderate, individuals with hypertension should be aware that regular daily consumption of multiple kopitiam coffees may contribute meaningfully to sodium intake, and caffeine can transiently elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar from condensed milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: A large Ice Kopi delivers approximately 31 g of sugar per 400 ml serving, predominantly from sweetened condensed milk; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage, or request a reduced-sugar or sugar-free version (Kopi Siu Dai or Kopi O).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed and evaporated milk, and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~4 g per serving) and high sugar (~31 g per serving) in a large Ice Kopi warrants moderation for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease, as chronic high sugar intake is associated with elevated triglycerides and endothelial dysfunction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice Kopi (Large) is a traditional Singaporean iced coffee beverage served in a large cup (approximately 400 ml), prepared in the Nanyang kopitiam style. It is made by brewing strong, dark-roasted Robusta coffee beans — typically roasted with butter and sugar in the traditional Nanyang method — through a cloth sock filter, then combining the brew with sweetened condensed milk and evaporated milk, and pouring it over ice. The result is a rich, intensely aromatic, creamy, and sweet iced coffee with a characteristic bittersweet flavour profile that distinguishes it from Western-style iced lattes or cold brews. The condensed milk provides sweetness and a thick, silky mouthfeel, while the evaporated milk adds a lighter creaminess. Ice Kopi is a cultural staple of Singapore's kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) culture and is widely available at hawker centres, coffee shops, and chain outlets such as Toast Box, Ya Kun Kaya Toast, and Killiney Kopitiam. Nutritionally, a large serving contains approximately 208 kcal, 5.6 g protein, 6 g fat, and 32.8 g carbohydrate, with the majority of carbohydrate coming from added sugars in the condensed milk. The beverage has a low glycemic index (~41) but a moderate glycemic load (~13.4) due to the serving size. It is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without modification. Clinically, the high sugar content is the primary concern for metabolic health, while the moderate sodium and saturated fat content are relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Ice Kopi large?
Sugar content of Kopi with condensed milk
Difference between Kopi and Kopi C
How much sugar in kopitiam coffee?

confidence_reason: HPB Singapore Foodo provides a standard Kopi entry with condensed and evaporated milk, which is the closest approved primary source. Per-100g values are adapted from the standard hot Kopi serving with an ice-dilution adjustment. No exact large iced serving entry exists in HPB Foodo; therefore confidence is medium rather than high. USDA FoodData Central used as cross-reference for condensed milk and evaporated milk macros.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo standard Kopi (hot, ~200 ml) with condensed and evaporated milk, adjusted for approximately 12% ice-dilution typical of iced kopitiam beverages. 2. Serving size set at 400 g (400 ml) consistent with a large cup as sold at Toast Box and similar outlets. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as coffee beverages with milk contain no dietary fibre per HPB and USDA sources. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero; no hydrogenated fats are used in standard kopitiam kopi preparation. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this beverage category. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this beverage category. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this beverage category. 8. glycemic_index set to 41 (low) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database condensed milk and milk beverage data; no direct GI measurement exists for Nanyang iced kopi. 9. GL per large serving = 41 × (8.2 g carb per 100g × 400g / 100) / 100 = 41 × 32.8 / 100 = 13.4. 10. Kopi ordering variants: Kopi (condensed milk), Kopi C (evaporated milk only), Kopi O (black with sugar), Kopi Siu Dai (less sweet), Kopi Kosong (no sugar). This record covers the standard Kopi formulation with both condensed and evaporated milk served iced in a large cup.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from HPB Singapore Foodo source. Identified that dietary_fibre_g=0.0 and trans_fat_g=0.0 required zero-justification documentation in notes.

Iteration 2: Added explicit zero-justification phrases in notes for dietary_fibre_g and trans_fat_g. Added sentinel value documentation for vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all ontology match_type values use only allowed enum values (exact/closest_parent/none). Confirmed GI > 0 with numeric value 41. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 verified as passing.
